refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational scenarios.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials (e.g., standardized earthmoving scenarios) with both on-site and remote operators; measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) against target; log safety incidents and policy violations; correlate productivity with operator mode, task complexity, and environmental conditions.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with precision and repeatability sufficient to achieve target throughput.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness (on-site or remote) with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task tracking to enable productivity measurement and operator decision-making.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-precision autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time teleoperation overlay, safety interlock system, and continuous telemetry streaming to achieve target productivity while enforcing safety constraints.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle control architecture with pluggable task execution engines (dig, load, dump), operator interface abstraction (on-site/remote switchable), and safety service middleware that monitors and gates task execution.

DP-C: Implement a productivity-centric design where vehicle actuation, teleoperation, and safety services are co-designed as a unified control loop: operator intent → task planner → vehicle controller → telemetry feedback → safety validator → productivity meter.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle control architecture with pluggable task execution engines (dig, load, dump), operator interface abstraction (on-site/remote switchable), and safety service middleware that monitors and gates task execution.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle actuation and teleoperation latency: mitigated by operator interface abstraction layer that decouples physical control from logical task intent.
Coupling between safety services and productivity: mitigated by safety middleware that enforces constraints without blocking task execution (e.g., speed limiting vs. task blocking).
Coupling between task execution engines and vehicle hardware: mitigated by pluggable architecture that allows task engines to be swapped without vehicle controller changes.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids tight coupling by introducing abstraction layers (operator interface, safety middleware, task engine interface). This supports both on-site and remote operation modes and allows safety constraints to be applied orthogonally to task execution.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator interface abstraction can achieve latency parity between on-site and remote modes; Risk: Remote teleoperation may introduce unacceptable latency that degrades productivity.
Assumption: Safety middleware can enforce constraints without blocking critical task paths; Risk: Over-conservative safety rules may prevent operators from achieving target productivity.
Assumption: Pluggable task execution engines can be validated independently; Risk: Integration testing may reveal unforeseen interactions between task engines and vehicle dynamics.
Assumption: Telemetry streaming is reliable and sufficient for real-time productivity measurement; Risk: Network failures or sensor drift may corrupt productivity metrics.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., <200ms round-trip for remote mode), (2) safety constraint classification (hard stops vs. soft limits), (3) task engine interface specification (command/feedback protocol), (4) telemetry data model and sampling rates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to deliver the productivity KPI. It owns the overall control loop, task scheduling, telemetry aggregation, and productivity measurement.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-level task directives from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B'); NGHE Solution translates to vehicle-level commands and task engine invocations.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Streams real-time vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, fuel, temperature), task progress (% complete, volume moved), and productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) to Site Manager and Tele-operator dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits safety events (e.g., geofence breach, operator override, equipment fault, policy violation) to Safety Manager and on-site safety personnel; may trigger task suspension or vehicle shutdown.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication backbone for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert; supports both on-site (local network) and remote (WAN) connectivity; latency and reliability are critical for teleoperation and safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the productivity KPI. It owns the control loop, task orchestration, and telemetry aggregation. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module owns actuation and low-level control but does not own teleoperation, safety services, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Vehicle would diffuse teleoperation and safety logic across multiple modules.

Teleoperation module owns operator interface and remote control but does not own vehicle actuation, safety enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Teleoperation would diffuse vehicle control and safety logic.

Safety Services module owns constraint enforcement and safety monitoring but does not own vehicle actuation, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Safety Services would diffuse task execution and telemetry logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the productivity KPI because it integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services into a unified control loop. The module owns task orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and productivity measurement—the key mechanisms for achieving the KPI. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute specialized capabilities but do not independently own the KPI.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too much logic and becomes difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Define clear interfaces between NGHE Solution and sub-modules; use dependency injection and event-driven patterns to decouple concerns.
Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be duplicated or inconsistent across modules. Mitigation: Centralize productivity calculation in NGHE Solution; expose as a service to other modules.
Risk: Telemetry aggregation may introduce latency or data loss. Mitigation: Define telemetry data model and sampling rates; implement buffering and retry logic in Network Link module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine IM assignment to specify: (1) NGHE Solution internal architecture (e.g., task scheduler, telemetry aggregator, productivity meter as distinct components), (2) interface contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (e.g., Vehicle command/feedback protocol, Teleoperation operator intent format, Safety Services constraint API), (3) ownership of telemetry data model and productivity calculation algorithm.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly specifies the productivity outcome (≥ productivity_target m³/h or ton/h) and the constraints (safety plan, operational policies). DP-B (modular vehicle control with pluggable task engines, operator interface abstraction, and safety middleware) directly addresses the FR by enabling task execution, operator control (on-site/remote), and safety enforcement. The DP is logically sound and avoids unnecessary coupling.

diffusion_risks: Risk: Task execution engines (dig, load, dump) may be implemented in Vehicle module, but task orchestration and scheduling logic may be in NGHE Solution, creating diffusion of task execution responsibility. Mitigation: Define clear task engine interface and ownership boundary.

Risk: Teleoperation operator interface abstraction may be implemented in Teleoperation module, but task intent translation may be in NGHE Solution, creating diffusion of operator intent handling. Mitigation: Define operator intent data model and translation protocol.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for the DP because it owns the control loop and task orchestration. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that implement specialized capabilities (actuation, operator interface, constraint enforcement) but do not independently own the productivity KPI. The IM boundary is clear and well-defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable through representative operations trials that measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h), safety compliance, and operator satisfaction. Telemetry and task tracking data from NGHE Solution can be logged and analyzed post-trial. Verification is feasible with standard testing methodologies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The FR is allocated to NGHE Solution, which owns the control loop, task orchestration, and productivity measurement. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are allocated sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1, FR-NGHE-001.2, FR-NGHE-001.3, FR-NGHE-001.4) that contribute to the overall KPI. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine the productivity target definition: specify units (m³/h or ton/h), measurement period (e.g., 8-hour shift), and acceptable variance (e.g., ±10%).
Define the representative operations scenarios: specify task types (dig, load, dump), worksite conditions (terrain, weather), and operator experience levels.
Specify the safety plan and operational policies that must be enforced: e.g., geofence limits, speed limits, operator override rules, equipment fault responses.
Define the telemetry data model and sampling rates: specify which vehicle state variables are measured, at what frequency, and with what accuracy.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and the DP is logically sound, but the IM assignment to NGHE Solution requires further decomposition to clarify the internal architecture and interface contracts. The sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are ready for the next level of functional decomposition, where each sub-FR will be mapped to a DP and assigned to a sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Proceeding to the next level will validate the modular architecture and identify any coupling or diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with precision and repeatability sufficient to achieve target throughput.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness (on-site or remote) with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task tracking to enable productivity measurement and operator decision-making.

assumptions: Operator interface abstraction can achieve latency parity between on-site and remote modes.
Safety middleware can enforce constraints without blocking critical task paths.
Pluggable task execution engines can be validated independently.
Telemetry streaming is reliable and sufficient for real-time productivity measurement.
Productivity target is achievable with current vehicle and operator capabilities.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and stable.
open_risks: Remote teleoperation may introduce unacceptable latency that degrades productivity.
Over-conservative safety rules may prevent operators from achieving target productivity.
Integration testing may reveal unforeseen interactions between task engines and vehicle dynamics.
Network failures or sensor drift may corrupt productivity metrics.
NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too much logic.
Productivity measurement logic may be duplicated or inconsistent across modules.
evidence_needed: Latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., <200ms round-trip for remote mode).
Safety constraint classification (hard stops vs. soft limits).
Telemetry data model and sampling rates.
Representative operations scenarios and acceptance criteria.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies documentation.
Proof-of-concept or prototype results for teleoperation and safety middleware.
